title: Tom Lee's Small-Cap Granny Shots ETF Reloads: First Solar, Zoom Among 10 New Buys
author: Chandrima Sanyal
description: Fundstrat Capital completed its May 2026 quarterly rebalance of the Fundstrat Granny Shots US Small- & Mid-Cap ETF (GRNJ), adding 10 stocks including First Solar, Zoom, Credo Technology, and Neurocrine Biosciences, while removing 8 positions. CIO Tom Lee positions the portfolio for a 'challenging but ultimately constructive' environment, with the strategy focusing on companies benefiting from structural tailwinds across themes like Millennials, Global Labor Supply, Energy, Cybersecurity, and Easing Financial Conditions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Active rebalancing with strategic additions of quality companies across growth sectors; positioned for constructive market environment despite near-term challenges
Added to portfolio in rebalance, indicating alignment with renewable energy structural tailwinds
New addition to portfolio, likely benefiting from cybersecurity and technology themes
Added to portfolio, indicating healthcare sector positioning aligned with fund themes
Removed from portfolio as it no longer met thematic criteria
Removed from portfolio, indicating it no longer aligns with fund's investment themes

title: Nxera Pharma Operational Highlights and Consolidated Results for the First Quarter 2026
author: Nxera Pharma
description: Nxera Pharma reported strong Q1 2026 results with revenue of JPY 11.3 billion, a significant increase from the prior year, driven by seven R&D milestone events. The company signed a licensing agreement with Santhera for vamorolone in APAC markets, achieved positive Phase 3 results for daridorexant in South Korea, and received milestone payments from Neurocrine and AbbVie. Additionally, Centessa Pharmaceuticals (spun out from Nxera in 2019) agreed to be acquired by Eli Lilly for up to $7.8 billion, with Nxera retaining rights to milestone and royalty payments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Advanced muscarinic agonist portfolio with multiple ongoing Phase 2/3 studies, achieved milestone triggering US$22.5 million payment to Nxera, and initiated Phase 2 trial for NBI-1117570 in schizophrenia.

Acquired Centessa Pharmaceuticals for up to $7.8 billion, gaining access to promising OX2R agonist pipeline (cleminorexton/ORX750, ORX142, ORX489) discovered using Nxera's proprietary NxStaR technology.
Agreed to be acquired by Eli Lilly for up to $7.8 billion, representing significant value creation for the company spun out from Nxera in 2019 with its orexin receptor agonist program.
Achieved third milestone under neurological diseases collaboration with Nxera, resulting in US$10 million payment, indicating steady progress but limited detail on strategic significance.

title: Nxera Pharma’s Partner Neurocrine Biosciences Doses First Patient in Phase 2 Trial of NBI-1117570 for Adults with Schizophrenia
author: Na
description: Neurocrine Biosciences has initiated a Phase 2 clinical trial of NBI-1117570, a dual muscarinic M1/M4 receptor agonist discovered by Nxera Pharma, for treating schizophrenia. Nxera will receive $22.5 million in milestone payments from Neurocrine as a result of this achievement.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Neurocrine successfully initiated a Phase 2 trial for NBI-1117570, marking the second NxWave-designed compound in their muscarinic agonist portfolio to advance to Phase 2. This demonstrates progress in their schizophrenia treatment program and validates their partnership strategy.

title: Why Soleno Therapeutics Stock Rocketed Higher on Monday
author: Eric Volkman
description: Soleno Therapeutics agreed to be acquired by peer Neurocrine Biosciences for $2.9 billion, or $53 per share in an all-cash deal, representing a 34% premium. The acquisition is driven by Soleno's FDA-approved drug Vykat XR for treating hyperphagia in Prader-Willi Syndrome patients. Soleno's stock surged 32% on the announcement, and the deal is expected to close within 90 days.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 32% on acquisition announcement at a 34% premium to previous trading price. The company's FDA-approved product and rare disease focus made it an attractive acquisition target for Neurocrine.

Stock showed minimal movement (+0.62%) despite the acquisition announcement. While the deal supports portfolio diversification and revenue growth strategy, the market reaction was muted, suggesting neutral sentiment on the acquisition's immediate impact.

title: Neurocrine Buys Soleno To Tap High-Growth Rare Disease Market
author: Vandana Singh
description: Neurocrine Biosciences announced its acquisition of Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion ($53 per share in cash) to expand its rare disease portfolio with Vykat XR, an FDA-approved treatment for hyperphagia in Prader-Willi syndrome. The deal is expected to close within 90 days. Vykat XR generated $190 million in revenue in 2025, demonstrating strong market adoption despite earlier criticism from a short report.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the acquisition is strategically sound and adds a high-growth product with strong revenue generation ($190M in 2025), the stock declined 2.74% in premarket trading. Technical analysis shows bearish long-term signals (trading 4.5% below 200-day SMA), though the company has a solid 12-month return of 41.13% and strong growth profile.

Soleno shares surged 32.84% in premarket trading following the acquisition announcement at $53 per share, representing a significant premium. The company's flagship product Vykat XR has demonstrated strong commercial success and market validation through FDA approval and robust early adoption.
